title: Audio Player
description: With the Audio Player function block you can define an Audio Zone. This is usually a room or an area of a building where audio playback is desired. The block is used with the outputs of the Audioserver or a Stereo Extension.

inputs.description: Increase volume by value set in parameter (Vsts). Double-click selects next favorite.
Decrease volume by value set in parameter (Vsts). Double-click turns off the player.
If the player is turned off, playback will start automatically.
Selects a favorite based on its assigned ID number. If the selected ID does not exist, the first favorite is selected.
Plays alarm sound at volume set in parameter (Va).
Plays fire alarm sound at volume set in parameter (Va).
Plays doorbell sound at volume set in parameter (Vbell).
Starts alarm clock action as specified in the properties. If the action "Alarm Clock Chime" is selected, the alarm clock sound is played at the volume set in parameter (Vbuzzer).
Selects the Line In of an Audioserver as source based on its Line In ID.
Pulse: Outputs are reset / switched off. On: Block is locked. Dominating input. The name of the connected sensor is used in the user interface.
Disables inputs V+, V-, V, Play, Pause, Prev, Next, Fav, Bell, Buzzer, T5, TTS, Cs, BTp when On. (e.g child lock, cleaning) Control via user interface is still possible.
As long as this input is active, any change in value at input (P) is ignored.

Button 2 : Volume up; double-click starts playback or choses next favorite Button 5 : Volume down; double-click pauses playback Button 3: Double-click activates (2C); triple-click activates (3C); (Roff) = 0: pauses playback
Converts a Text to speech and plays it at the volume set in parameter (Vtts).
Resets parameters and settings of the block to the default values as specified in the block preset.

Plays custom sound [filename] at volume [vol]. E.g. soundcheck.mp3:80 [filename]:[vol] Custom sounds have to be stored on the Audioserver’s SD card in the folder Event_Sounds! Only mp3 files are supported.

outputs.description: Pulse on a double- or triple-click or pulse on input (Off).
Outputs the left channel of the stereo signal.
Outputs the right channel of the stereo signal.
Functionality is only enabled when Stereo Outputs with external controlled "Volume-Mode" are connected.
Functionality is only enabled when Stereo Outputs with external controlled "Volume-Mode" are connected.
Outputs the subwoofer channel of the stereo signal. This output can only be used with Master/Client subwoofers.

As long as this output is active, Bluetooth pairing is active. During this time all Bluetooth-capable Loxone devices can be paired with a smartphone or other Bluetooth devices. This output is only visible in certain configurations.
Intelligent API based connector. API Commands

parameters.description: Volume when playback is started or resumed. -1 = Saves the last volume setting as the startup volume.
Decrease volume by value set in parameter (Vsts). Double-click turns off the player.
If the current playback volume is higher, it is used instead of the minimum volume.
If the current playback volume is higher, it is used instead of the minimum volume.
If the current playback volume is higher, it is used instead of the minimum volume.
If the current playback volume is higher, it is used instead of the minimum volume.
Ignore Room Off / House Off via T5 (button 3)
Room favorite ID for alarm clock, if the alarm clock action room favorite is used.

As long as this input is active, bluetooth pairing is available. During this time all bluetooth-enabled Loxone devices can be paired with e.g. a smartphone or other bluetooth-enabled devices. This parameter is only visible in certain configurations.

Volume adjustments made via input V are applied within the configured time when the player is already active. The fading time also applies to the Buzzer input. 0 = No fading; volume changes occur immediately.

properties.description: Action that is carried out when the alarm goes off
Enable AirPlay for this player. Option is not available if music services are handled by Player-Group.
Enable Spotify Connect for this player. Option is not available if music services are handled by Player-Group.
If checked, the first room favorite will always be used when the player is activated.
Number of entries in the activity log. 0: log is disabled The activity log tracks relevant changes since program start.
ID of the player on the Audioserver or audio device

When a subwoofer is connected to the Audio Player, all other speakers automatically stop reproducing bass frequencies, reducing their load. This behavior can be disabled by unchecking the corresponding checkbox, allowing all connected speakers to play the full frequency range.
